title: Iran Receives Trump's 15-Point Peace Plan; Vance and Rubio Participating in Talks
detail: Iran confirmed receipt of President Trump's 15-point peace plan to end the ongoing US-Iran war, delivered via intermediaries through Islamabad, Pakistan, according to the Associated Press. Trump stated on March 24, 2026 that the US and Iran are 'in negotiations right now,' and said he backed off threats to strike Iranian energy infrastructure 'based on the fact we're negotiating.' VP Vance and Secretary of State Rubio are participating in talks alongside Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner. Defense Secretary Hegseth was described by Trump as 'quite disappointed' by the ceasefire negotiations.

media.caption: Iran receives Trump's 15-point peace plan via Pakistan intermediary

title: Congressional Leaders Voice Frustration Over Lack of Iran War Transparency
detail: Members of both the House and Senate Armed Services Committees expressed frustration following a classified Pentagon briefing on Iran war operations. House Armed Services Committee Chairman Rep. Mike Rogers stated: 'We want to know more about what's going on, what the options are and why they're being considered. And we're just not getting enough answers on those questions.' A Reuters/Ipsos poll found 61% of Americans disapproving of US military strikes in Iran; a Pew poll found 59% calling the decision wrong; a Quinnipiac poll found 54% of registered voters opposed.
